title: Banks Flip Your Transactions to Drain $12 Billion
hook: Your bank secretly reorders your transactions largest-to-smallest — one quiet trick that cost Americans $12 billion in overdraft fees last year.
angle: Most people think overdraft fees are their own fault. They're not — banks deliberately sort debits high-to-low to maximize the number of overdraft triggers on a single day.
audience: Anyone with a checking account who has ever been hit with an unexpected overdraft fee
key_numbers: $12 billion collected in overdraft fees by US banks in 2023
High-to-low transaction reordering can turn 1 overdraft into 5 on a single day
Average overdraft fee: $35 — charged per transaction, not per day
